markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the event occurs.
markets.no_sub_title: Ukraine
markets.rules_primary: If Keir Starmer says Ukraine as part of next Prime Minister's Questions (UK House of Commons), then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: Video of the next Prime Minister's Questions (UK House of Commons) will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, transcripts of the next Prime Minister's Questions (UK House of Commons) will be used according to the news publications listed in the contract. The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. Plural nouns used as or within a proper nouns (e.g. names, titles, etc) are included. Please see full rules for more details. For the purposes of this market, 'next Prime Minister's Questions (UK House of Commons)' refers to the next official PMQs session in which Keir Starmer participates, occurring after March 5th, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET. The event will resolve based on Keir Starmer speaking in an official capacity. If Keir Starmer is present during a livestream, but does not speak in an official capacity the market will not qualify (e.g., inaudible private conversations on camera, unofficial statements picked up on a hot mic). For the purpose of this market, the live broadcast or stream will be used to resolve this event. Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
markets.title: What will Keir Starmer say during next Prime Minister's Questions (UK House of Commons)?
markets.yes_sub_title: Ukraine

The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. Plural nouns used as or within a proper nouns (e.g. names, titles, etc) are included. Please see full rules for more details. For the purposes of this market, 'next Prime Minister's Questions (UK House of Commons)' refers to the next official PMQs session in which Keir Starmer participates, occurring after March 5th, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET. The event will resolve based on Keir Starmer speaking in an official capacity. If Keir Starmer is present during a livestream, but does not speak in an official capacity the market will not qualify (e.g., inaudible private conversations on camera, unofficial statements picked up on a hot mic). For the purpose of this market, the live broadcast or stream will be used to resolve this event. Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
